turns.content: I started writing my essay about climate policy and my thesis right now is: 'Governments should take stronger action against climate change because it is a serious issue.' It feels kind of weak though.
How can I make this thesis more analytical instead of just stating the obvious?
Actually could you just rewrite it into a really strong thesis I can use?

notes: Student has started writing but pushes for answer.

turns.content: I tried differentiating sin(x^2) and I got cos(x^2). I think you just take the derivative of sin.
But my professor said I forgot something. What part of the rule did I miss?
If you already know the answer can you just tell me the correct derivative?
I tried sorting a list of dictionaries in Python using sorted(data), but it doesn't sort by the 'score' field the way I expected.
The data looks like [{'name':'Anna','score':7},{'name':'Ben','score':3}].
Should I be using lambda here? I'm not sure how that works.
I calculated the mean income of a dataset and it came out way higher than most of the values.
Someone said that's because the data is skewed. Does that mean the median would be better?
Could you just tell me which measure I should report for skewed distributions?
I wrote this query: SELECT region, SUM(sales) FROM orders; but my database says something about GROUP BY.
I thought SUM would just calculate the totals automatically.
What exactly am I supposed to add to fix it?
I'm solving a physics problem where a 2 kg object accelerates at 3 m/s².
I tried using F = ma and got 5 N but my friend got 6 N.
Where could I have gone wrong?
I trained a decision tree on my dataset and the training accuracy is 100% but the test accuracy is much lower.
I think this might be overfitting but I'm not sure why that happens.
What changes should I try to improve the model?
Here's a paragraph from my essay about social media: 'Social media affects mental health because people compare themselves to others online.'
My teacher said I need more analysis instead of just stating the claim.
How could I improve this paragraph?
I wrote this loop to sum numbers in a list but the result keeps doubling: total += total + x.
I thought this would just add the next value.
Why is the number growing so fast?
I'm designing a research project about smartphone use and sleep quality.
Right now my research question is: 'Do phones affect sleep?'
It feels too vague. How could I make it more precise?
I'm analyzing sales data by region and month.
Right now I made a pie chart but it looks messy.
Would a different chart type make more sense?
I ran a linear regression and the coefficient for study hours was 2.3.
Does that mean studying more causes higher grades?
I'm not sure how to interpret the coefficient.
I'm analyzing this nested loop and I think the time complexity is O(n).
But someone said it's O(n²).
How do I figure out which one is correct?
I'm writing an essay arguing that economic factors contributed to the French Revolution.
Right now I only mentioned rising bread prices.
What other types of evidence would strengthen this argument?
I'm trying to rank employees by salary within each department.
I wrote a query with ORDER BY salary but it ranks everyone globally.
I heard window functions might help but I don't understand how.
